steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: The terms $3x^3$ and $9$ share a greatest common factor of $3$. The variable $x$ is not common to both terms because it appears only in the first term.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: Factor $3$ out of both terms. Dividing $3x^3$ by $3$ leaves $x^3$, and dividing $9$ by $3$ leaves $3$: $$ 3x^3+9=3(x^3+3) $$ The answer is D.
